behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a module handoff boundary (collectors -> report assembler -> summary input), not inside one pure function. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: real network/OS is not required; in-process fakes for the three source clients can expose missing wiring or dropped fields. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: a Unit test of only the payload-builder function could pass with handcrafted input while production still fails because one source adapter is never wired into orchestration. REJECTION-GATE: I considered System, but behavior text is specifically about "content passed to the summary-generation step," not real HTTP/subprocess behavior; that disqualifies System as minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real orchestration modules with three fake source adapters returning distinct sentinel values; run the pipeline up to the summary-generation call boundary and assert the outgoing summary input contains all three source sections and sentinels.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is cross-module error propagation (source adapter failure -> section fallback text -> overall report still produced). REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: a controlled adapter failure in-process is sufficient; no real OS/network behavior is required to reproduce the fallback logic failure. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: a Unit test of the formatter that inserts "data unavailable" can pass, while orchestration still aborts because adapter exceptions are not caught at integration boundaries. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess for this behavior itself, (b) correctness does not depend on AI output quality, (c) no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run report generation with two source modules returning normal data and one source module raising a representative availability exception; assert final report is emitted, includes a `data unavailable` note in only the failed section, and does not terminate early.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on the model's actual summarization decisions (value fidelity), which a stub cannot validate. MOCK-HIDE: mocked/deterministic summaries can always pass even when real model outputs omit or alter key values. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: a System-level test with real HTTP/email but a stubbed summary generator passes, while production fails because the real model hallucinates or misstates a key metric. REJECTION-GATE: I considered Workflow, but behavior text names a single "summary-generation" quality check, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real summary-generation model on a curated dataset suite with known key values; score outputs for factual value preservation (e.g., exact numbers/entities present), and fail when accuracy drops below threshold across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this boundary includes real external delivery and inbox receipt, which requires real HTTP/API + real mail transport behavior. MOCK-HIDE: mocked email clients can pass while real SMTP/API auth, TLS, routing, or inbox delivery semantics fail. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: an Integration test with a fake mail sender passes, but real delivery fails due to SMTP/API configuration or transport-level rejection, so inbox is never populated. REJECTION-GATE: I considered Agentic, but behavior text targets "reaches ... inbox correctly" (delivery outcome), not LLM output quality; that disqualifies Agentic as the minimum for this specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run the script end-to-end against reachable test instances of the three data sources and a real test email service/account; verify one generated report email arrives in the configured recipient inbox with expected subject/body structure.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior depends on real delivery unreachability characteristics plus persistence across runs (filesystem + scheduler/run boundary). MOCK-HIDE: a mock mail client that raises a simplified exception can miss real provider/network failure modes and retry-path integration bugs across process boundaries. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: an Integration test with mocked send-failure passes fallback logic, but in production the real mail library raises a different exception class, so report is neither persisted nor retried on next run. REJECTION-GATE: I considered Workflow, but behavior does not require multiple agentic decisions; it is infrastructure retry/persistence across runs.
behaviors.test_description: Execute two real runs: first with email endpoint intentionally unreachable, assert report is written to configured local fallback path; second run with endpoint restored, assert persisted report is retried/sent and fallback artifact/state is cleared or marked delivered per design.
